subject: LU-4665 utils: lfs setstripe to specify OSTs

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(6 comments)

Since this is closely tied to the Layout Enhancement project, I think it is critical to get feedback from John before this goes any further.

Also, it makes sense to implement this in the framework provided by http://review.whamcloud.com/5302 so that we don't get duplicate APIs for doing nearly the same thing.

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

(17 comments)

Most *param paramaters should be const.

I don't think we should ask for permission to use a given target in llapi. Rather we should take the list of targets the user supplied and send that to the MDT.

We also shouldn't sort the list. (It's OK to sort a copy to check for uniqueness of the indexes.)

Patch Set 5:

(17 comments)

It makes coding extremely difficult because there is no standard for what's acceptable coding style.

Patch Set 5:

(4 comments)

Function names should reflect what they do, not what you're using them for.

See https://wiki.hpdd.intel.com/display/PUB/Coding+Guidelines

Patch Set 10: Code-Review-1

(13 comments)

Still some changes I'd like (at minimum using lov_mds_md_v3 instead of lov_mds_md_v1 so that it is at least _possible_ to add pool support later), because this is part of the wire protocol and it is hard to change this after it is in a release (and realistically nobody will take time to fix it after it is landed).

Also, it is fine if Yu Jian adds test cases for this feature, but she should include them as part of this patch, or we have no way to know it is currently working and continues to work.

Has this been tested against an old MDS to make sure that it doesn't do something foolish like LASSERT() upon seeing LOV_USER_MAGIC_V5?  The tests might want run against older MDSes, but not fail the test if "lfs setstripe" returns an error on an older MDS, just to verify the old MDS doesn't die.

Patch Set 10:

(7 comments)

Yes, yujian is working on test cases right now.

Good point on older MDSes, I will verify it.

I will work a new patch tomorrow.

Patch Set 10:

(5 comments)

Patch Set 10:

I've created patch http://review.whamcloud.com/9722 to improve Lustre test framework to support specifying arbitrary OST indices. I'll make that patch based on this patch and add regression test case into conf-sanity to validate this patch.

Patch Set 16:

Hi James,

Please also merge the following changes into your patch:

diff --git a/lustre/utils/lfs.c b/lustre/utils/lfs.c
index c358d9d..aa3ba5a 100644
--- a/lustre/utils/lfs.c
+++ b/lustre/utils/lfs.c
@@ -1980,6 +1980,9 @@ static int mntdf(char *mntdir, char *fsname, char *pool, int ishow,
                         if (rc == -ENODEV)
                                 break;
 
+                       if (rc == -EAGAIN)
+                               continue;
+
                         if (poolname && tp->st_op == LL_STATFS_LOV &&
                             llapi_search_ost(fsname, poolname,
                                              obd_uuid2str(&uuid_buf)) != 1)

This is to fix a problem that `lfs df' printed lots of non-existing OST with `Resource Temporarily Unavailable'.

Thanks

Patch Set 17:

I saw the following problem once when I was running the test:

/home/jxiong/srcs/lustre/lustre/tests/../utils/lfs setstripe -o 42178,7774,8602 /mnt/lustre/d82a.conf-sanity/f82a.conf-sanity-1
llapi_file_open_param: cannot find OST lustre-OST1e5e_UUID in system: No such device (19)
error: setstripe: create stripe file '/mnt/lustre/d82a.conf-sanity/f82a.conf-sanity-1' failed
 conf-sanity test_82a: @@@@@@ FAIL: /home/jxiong/srcs/lustre/lustre/tests/../utils/lfs setstripe -o 42178,7774,8602 /mnt/lustre/d82a.conf-sanity/f82a.conf-sanity-1 failed 


It couldn't find the corresponding OST from /proc/fs/lustre/lov/*/target_obd. I'll take a further look.

Patch Set 17:

it turned out glob(3) always picked /proc/fs/lustre/lov/lustre-MDT0000-mdtlov to find OBD targets, where we should use clilov instead. I'm working on a patch to fix it.

Patch Set 17:

Looks like this patch ends up fixing several hidden bugs that exist.

Patch Set 24:

lfs currently accepts stripe counts that are inconsistent with the OST range, e.g.

 lfs setstripe -c 8 -o 1-3

I think this should either be an error (better), or the man page should document that -c is ignored if -o is specified (worse).

Patch Set 24:

I will leave this patch in this state for several days please spend enough time to find all of style issues so that I can fix them in the next revision. I have made at least 5 revisions to fix those style issues but there are still pretty of them. Most of them exist since the 1st revision of the patch. Really tired of doing this.

I'll appreciate if you guys can help me find any `real' problems of the patch.

Patch Set 24:

Specifying -i -1 with a specific OST list results in this error message:

 llapi_file_open_param: stripe offset '-1' is not in the target list: Invalid argument (22)

This case should be handled more sensibly, and the man page should document how -i interacts with -o.  Same goes for -c.

Patch Set 24:

(2 comments)

Patch Set 24:

These are the semantics of -i and -c when they are interacting with -o:

-i <offset>: the offset must be in the OST list;
-c <stripe_count>: this option is omitted with -o is indicated.

Ned, Can you please compose a paragraph about this and I'll merge it in the next revision? Really don't want to challenge it with my 3rd grade English.

Jinshan

Patch Set 24:

Jinshan, I'd be happy to do that and will post something tomorrow. Do you plan to make lfs return an error if -c is combined with -o, or just continue to ignore it? That will change the wording of the description. My preference is to return an error.

Patch Set 24:

Right now in this patch, -c is ignored. I'm fine to change it so if -c doesn't match the number of OSTs in the list, it will return an error.

Patch Set 24:

Proposed man page wording to document the -o option.
 
 The
 .B -o
 option is used to specify the exact stripe layout on the file system.
 .I ost_indices
 is a list of OSTs referenced by their indices, which are specified in decimal
 form an can be obtained using the
 .B lfs osts
 command.  The list format consists of individual OST indices and index
 ranges separated by commas, e.g. 1,2-4,7. The
 .B -o
 option may be specified multiple times to stripe across the union of
 all listed OSTs.
 If the
 .B -c
 option is combined with
 .B -o
 the
 .I stripe_count
 must agree with the number of OSTs in
 .IR ost_indices .
 If the
 .B -i
 option is combined with
 .B -o
 the
 .I start_ost_index
 must be in the OST list, and it will be used as the index to on which to
 start striping the file. Otherwise the striping will occur in the order
 specified in
 .IR ost_indices .

commit.subject: LU-4665 utils: lfs setstripe to specify OSTs
commit.message:
LU-4665 utils: lfs setstripe to specify OSTs

Extend lfs setstripe command to support specifying individual
OSTs. [--ost|-o] option is invented for this purpose. Support
specifying OSTs for regular files only. Directory support will
be implemented later in a separate project.

OSTs can be spcified by lfs setstripe [--ost|-o] as follows:
  lfs setstripe [[--ost-list|-o] <ost_1>,<ost_i>-<ost_j>,<ost_n>]

For example, -o 1,2,4-6,8 will be expanded as OSTs:
  1,2,4,5,6,8

In addition, duplicate indices will be eliminated automatically.

Calculate the max easize by ld_active_tgt_count instead of
ld_tgt_count. However this may introduce problems when the OSTs
are in recovery because non sufficient buffer may be allocated
to store EA.

This patch adds the following test cases into conf-sanity.sh:
test 81: sparse OST indexing
test 82a: specify OSTs for file (succeed) or directory (fail)
test 82b: specify OSTs for file with --pool and --ost-list options

Signed-off-by: Jian Yu <jian.yu@intel.com>
